description: Genotype-guided nutrition recommendations for precision biomarker optimization

entries.recommendation: Preformed EPA+DHA supplementation 2-4 g/day from fish oil or algal source. Cannot rely on plant ALA (flaxseed, chia, walnuts) for adequate EPA/DHA conversion.
avoid: Relying solely on plant-based ALA sources for omega-3
entries.text_chunk: FADS1 rs174547 CC genotype nutrition: Cannot efficiently convert plant ALA to EPA/DHA. Requires preformed EPA+DHA 2-4 g/day from fish oil or algal source. Target omega-3 index 8-12%. Eat fatty fish 3-4x/week (salmon, sardines, mackerel). Do NOT rely solely on flaxseed, chia, or walnuts for omega-3 needs.

entries.recommendation: Efficient ALA-to-EPA/DHA converter. Plant and marine sources both effective. Fish 2x/week plus 1 tbsp ground flaxseed or 1 oz walnuts daily.
entries.text_chunk: FADS1 rs174547 TT genotype nutrition: Efficient ALA converter. Both plant and marine omega-3 sources effective. Fish 2x/week plus flaxseed 1 tbsp/day or walnuts 1 oz/day. Target omega-3 index 8-12%. Can use plant-based sources as primary omega-3 strategy.

entries.recommendation: Methylfolate (5-MTHF) 1-5 mg/day instead of folic acid. MTHFR AA cannot efficiently convert folic acid to active form. Include folate-rich foods but supplement with methylfolate.
avoid: Folic acid supplements (synthetic form poorly converted by AA genotype)
Fortified foods with folic acid as sole folate source
entries.text_chunk: MTHFR rs1801133 AA (C677T TT) nutrition: Use methylfolate (5-MTHF) 1-5 mg/day, NOT folic acid. AA genotype has only ~30% enzyme activity for converting folic acid. Target homocysteine <10 umol/L. Dark leafy greens, lentils, liver (natural folate sources). Add methylcobalamin 1000 mcg/day and P5P (B6) 50 mg/day.

entries.recommendation: Standard folate intake 400-800 mcg DFE/day from food and/or folic acid supplements. Full enzyme activity; folic acid supplementation appropriate.
entries.text_chunk: MTHFR rs1801133 GG (C677T CC) nutrition: Standard folate 400-800 mcg DFE/day. Full MTHFR enzyme activity; folic acid or food folate both effective. Dark leafy greens, fortified grains, legumes, citrus. Standard homocysteine target <12 umol/L.

entries.recommendation: Higher vitamin D3 requirements: 2000-4000 IU/day. Target 25(OH)D >40 ng/mL (higher than standard 30 ng/mL). Combine with calcium 1000-1200 mg/day and magnesium 400 mg/day.
avoid: Relying solely on food sources (insufficient for AA genotype)
entries.text_chunk: VDR rs2228570 AA (FokI ff) nutrition: Higher vitamin D3 needs 2000-4000 IU/day. Target 25(OH)D >40 ng/mL (vs standard >30). Reduced VDR transcriptional activity requires higher levels. Fatty fish, egg yolks, UV-exposed mushrooms. Essential cofactors: calcium 1000-1200 mg/day, magnesium 400 mg/day. Food sources alone insufficient.

entries.recommendation: Total 25(OH)D may appear low but free/bioavailable vitamin D may be adequate due to lower VDBP. Consider free 25(OH)D testing before aggressive supplementation. Standard 1000-2000 IU/day usually sufficient.
entries.text_chunk: GC rs4588 AA nutrition: Lower vitamin D binding protein means total 25(OH)D appears 5-10 ng/mL lower but free vitamin D may be adequate. Request free 25(OH)D test before aggressive supplementation. Standard 1000-2000 IU/day usually sufficient. Do not over-supplement based on total measurement alone.

entries.recommendation: Strict saturated fat limitation <7% of calories (vs standard <10%). APOE4 carriers have exaggerated LDL response to saturated fat. Emphasize MUFA (olive oil) and omega-3 fats.
entries.text_chunk: APOE E4/E4 nutrition: Strict saturated fat <7% calories (not standard 10%). E4/E4 has exaggerated LDL response to saturated fat (+15-30 mg/dL). Use olive oil as primary fat. Avocado, nuts, fatty fish. Limit red meat to 1x/week. No butter, coconut oil, full-fat dairy, processed meats. Target LDL <100 (ideally <70).

entries.recommendation: Moderate saturated fat tolerance. Standard guidelines <10% of calories adequate. E2 carriers naturally have lower LDL. Focus on limiting refined carbohydrates (E2 prone to elevated triglycerides).
avoid: Excess refined carbohydrates (raise triglycerides in E2 carriers)
entries.text_chunk: APOE E2/E3 nutrition: Standard saturated fat <10% calories adequate. E2 naturally lowers LDL 10-15 mg/dL. Focus on limiting refined carbohydrates and sugar - E2 carriers prone to elevated triglycerides. Mediterranean diet pattern. Moderate dairy acceptable. Watch triglycerides more than LDL.

entries.recommendation: Restrict dietary iron: avoid iron-fortified foods, limit red meat to 1-2 servings/week, do not cook in cast iron. Avoid vitamin C supplements with meals (enhances iron absorption). Tea/coffee with meals reduces absorption.
food_sources: poultry and fish (lower iron than red meat)
tea or coffee with meals (inhibits iron absorption)
entries.text_chunk: HFE C282Y AA (homozygous) nutrition: Restrict dietary iron. Limit red meat to 1-2x/week. Avoid iron-fortified foods, cast iron cookware, vitamin C with meals (enhances absorption). Drink tea/coffee with meals (inhibits absorption). No iron supplements. No excess alcohol. Target ferritin 50-200 ng/mL with phlebotomy. Prefer poultry, fish, plant proteins.

entries.recommendation: Compound heterozygote: moderate iron awareness. Avoid iron supplements unless deficient. Annual ferritin monitoring. No strict dietary restriction needed unless ferritin rising.
food_sources: balanced diet without specific iron restriction
entries.text_chunk: HFE C282Y/H63D compound heterozygote nutrition: Moderate iron awareness. 1-2% clinical penetrance. No strict dietary restriction unless ferritin rising. Avoid unnecessary iron supplements. Annual ferritin monitoring. Moderate red meat acceptable. No excess alcohol.

entries.recommendation: Low glycemic index diet preferred. Limit refined carbohydrates. Distribute carbs across meals (avoid large carb loads). Pair carbs with protein/fat/fiber. Total carbs 40-45% of calories.
avoid: White bread, white rice, white pasta
entries.text_chunk: TCF7L2 rs7903146 TT nutrition: Low glycemic index diet critical. 1.88x T2D risk requires carbohydrate management. Limit refined carbs. Distribute carbs across meals with protein/fat/fiber pairing. Total carbs 40-45% calories. Non-starchy vegetables, legumes, intact whole grains, berries. Avoid white bread/rice/pasta, sugary drinks, fruit juice.

entries.recommendation: Standard carbohydrate guidelines: 45-55% of calories from carbs. Whole grains preferred over refined. No special restriction needed for T2D prevention from genotype alone.
avoid: Excess sugar and refined carbohydrates (general health)
entries.text_chunk: TCF7L2 rs7903146 CC nutrition: Standard carb guidelines 45-55% calories. Whole grains preferred. No genotype-specific restriction needed. Standard glucose targets. Focus on overall dietary quality.

entries.recommendation: Mediterranean diet strongly recommended. Strict alcohol avoidance (PNPLA3 GG + alcohol synergistically increases liver damage). Coffee 3-4 cups/day (hepatoprotective). Limit fructose. Weight management critical.
entries.text_chunk: PNPLA3 rs738409 GG nutrition: 3.26x NAFLD risk. Mediterranean diet essential. STRICT alcohol avoidance (PNPLA3 GG + alcohol synergistic liver damage). Coffee 3-4 cups/day (hepatoprotective). No fructose-sweetened drinks or HFCS. Olive oil as primary fat. Weight management critical (7-10% loss reverses steatosis). Use genotype-adjusted ALT upper limit 50 U/L.

entries.recommendation: Selenium 200 mcg/day from selenomethionine or Brazil nuts (1-2/day). DIO2 is a selenoenzyme; adequate selenium optimizes remaining T4-to-T3 conversion. Combine with adequate iodine 150 mcg/day.
avoid: Soy isoflavones in excess (may affect thyroid)
entries.text_chunk: DIO2 rs225014 AA nutrition: DIO2 is a selenoenzyme. Selenium 200 mcg/day optimizes remaining T4-to-T3 conversion. Brazil nuts 1-2/day (most concentrated food source). Adequate iodine 150 mcg/day (seaweed, iodized salt, dairy). Do not exceed selenium 400 mcg/day. Target free T3 2.5-4.4 pg/mL.

entries.recommendation: Selenium 100-200 mcg/day for antioxidant glutathione peroxidase optimization. TT carriers have reduced GPX1 activity. Important for thyroid protection in Hashimoto's.
avoid: Excess selenium >300 mcg/day with TT genotype
entries.text_chunk: GPX1 rs1050450 TT nutrition: Reduced glutathione peroxidase activity. Selenium 100-200 mcg/day for antioxidant optimization. Brazil nut 1/day. Important for thyroid protection in Hashimoto's context. Target serum selenium 100-150 ug/L. Do not exceed 300 mcg/day.

entries.recommendation: Slow caffeine metabolizer. Limit coffee to 1-2 cups/day. Avoid caffeine after noon. CC genotype associated with increased MI risk with >3 cups/day. May benefit from decaf or half-caf.
entries.text_chunk: CYP1A2 rs762551 CC (slow metabolizer) nutrition: Limit coffee to 1-2 cups/day, morning only. CC genotype has increased MI risk with >3 cups/day (slow caffeine clearance). Green tea acceptable (lower caffeine, L-theanine). No energy drinks. No caffeine after noon. Monitor blood pressure response.

entries.recommendation: Fast caffeine metabolizer. Coffee 3-4 cups/day associated with cardiovascular benefit and liver protection. No increased MI risk. Can tolerate afternoon caffeine.
entries.text_chunk: CYP1A2 rs762551 AA (fast metabolizer) nutrition: Coffee 3-4 cups/day is cardiovascular-protective and hepatoprotective. Rapid caffeine clearance eliminates MI risk. Can tolerate afternoon caffeine. Green tea and dark chocolate beneficial. Limit to <6 cups/day.

entries.recommendation: Increased salt sensitivity likely. Sodium restriction to <1500 mg/day (vs standard <2300 mg). DASH diet pattern. Higher potassium intake from fruits and vegetables.
entries.text_chunk: ACE rs4646994 DD nutrition: Higher ACE levels increase salt sensitivity. Sodium <1500 mg/day (stricter than 2300 mg standard). DASH diet pattern. High potassium from fruits/vegetables (bananas, potatoes, leafy greens). Avoid processed foods, restaurant meals, canned soups, deli meats. Target SBP <120 mmHg.

entries.recommendation: Reduced B12 absorption via intrinsic factor pathway. Sublingual methylcobalamin 1000 mcg/day recommended. Higher dietary B12 from animal sources. Monitor MMA as functional B12 marker.
avoid: Relying on low-B12 diet without supplementation
entries.text_chunk: FUT2 rs602662 AA nutrition: Reduced B12 absorption. Sublingual methylcobalamin 1000 mcg/day bypasses gut absorption issues. High-B12 foods: clams, liver, sardines, salmon. Target B12 >400 pg/mL. Monitor methylmalonic acid (MMA) as functional marker. Also check if on metformin (depletes B12).

entries.recommendation: Strict purine limitation. ABCG2 AA has reduced urate excretion increasing gout risk. Limit organ meats, shellfish, beer. Emphasize low-purine proteins. Cherry consumption may help.
entries.text_chunk: ABCG2 rs2231142 AA nutrition: Reduced urate excretion increases gout risk. Strict purine limitation. Avoid organ meats, shellfish, beer, HFCS. Low-fat dairy is protective. Tart cherry juice 8 oz/day reduces gout attacks. Coffee lowers uric acid. High-purine vegetables are NOT restricted. Target uric acid <6.0 mg/dL. Hydration 2+ L/day.

entries.recommendation: Strongly limit or avoid alcohol. ALDH2 deficiency causes acetaldehyde accumulation with facial flushing and dramatically increased esophageal cancer risk (6-10x if drinking).
entries.text_chunk: ALDH2 rs671 GA nutrition: STRONGLY limit or avoid all alcohol. Deficient acetaldehyde metabolism causes 6-10x esophageal cancer risk if drinking. Facial flushing is warning sign. No safe alcohol amount. Fermented foods (kimchi, yogurt) OK (negligible alcohol). Also affects nitroglycerin metabolism.

entries.recommendation: Increased dietary choline requirement. PEMT CC (especially women) have reduced endogenous choline synthesis. Target 550+ mg/day. Critical during pregnancy.
entries.text_chunk: PEMT rs12325817 CC nutrition: Reduced endogenous choline synthesis. Dietary choline 550+ mg/day required. Eggs 2/day (best source, ~300 mg). Liver excellent source. Critical during pregnancy (neural tube development). Vegan diets need choline supplementation. Consider phosphatidylcholine supplement if intake inadequate.

entries.recommendation: Higher magnesium requirements due to reduced intestinal absorption. Magnesium glycinate 400-600 mg/day. Important cofactor for vitamin D activation and glucose metabolism.
avoid: Excess calcium without magnesium (competes for absorption)
entries.text_chunk: TRPM6 rs11144134 TT nutrition: Reduced intestinal magnesium absorption. Magnesium glycinate 400-600 mg/day (not oxide - poor absorption). Cofactor for vitamin D activation and 300+ enzymes. Dark chocolate 85%+, pumpkin seeds, almonds, spinach. RBC magnesium more accurate than serum. Balance calcium:magnesium ratio 2:1.

entries.recommendation: Higher protein diet (25-30% of calories) to counteract reduced satiety. FTO AA carriers have impaired satiety signaling. Exercise attenuates FTO risk by 40% (aim 60+ min/day).
food_sources: lean proteins at every meal (eggs, fish, poultry, legumes)
entries.text_chunk: FTO rs9939609 AA nutrition: BMI +0.8 kg/m2 with reduced satiety. Higher protein 25-30% of calories to counter impaired satiety signaling. Protein at every meal. High-fiber, high-volume foods. Exercise 60+ min/day attenuates FTO risk 40%. Avoid ultra-processed foods. Mindful eating practices important.

entries.recommendation: Enhanced insulin sensitivity from Ala allele. Mediterranean diet particularly beneficial (amplifies genetic advantage). Moderate carbohydrate tolerance. Exercise synergizes with PPARG Ala for insulin sensitivity.
food_sources: moderate carbs from whole food sources
entries.text_chunk: PPARG rs1801282 CG/GG (Ala carriers) nutrition: Enhanced insulin sensitivity from protective allele. Mediterranean diet amplifies genetic advantage. Moderate carb tolerance from whole food sources. Exercise synergizes with PPARG Ala. Avoid trans fats (antagonize PPARG signaling). HOMA-IR target <2.0.

entries.recommendation: Paradoxically lower glucose but higher triglycerides. Limit refined carbohydrates and alcohol (both raise TG). Focus on omega-3 fats, fiber, and moderate carbs from whole food sources.
entries.text_chunk: GCKR rs1260326 TT nutrition: Genetically lower glucose but higher triglycerides +10-15 mg/dL, elevated CRP and uric acid. Limit refined carbs and alcohol. Omega-3 fats (fish, flaxseed). High fiber (oats, beans). Target TG <150. Also manage uric acid (see purine guidelines). Explains discordant glucose-lipid panels.

entries.recommendation: Higher calcium needs due to reduced VDR-mediated absorption. Calcium 1200-1500 mg/day from food + supplements. Vitamin K2 100-200 mcg/day for calcium directing to bone.
avoid: Very high protein diet without calcium compensation
entries.text_chunk: VDR rs2228570 AA (FokI ff) bone nutrition: Reduced calcium absorption. Calcium 1200-1500 mg/day from food + supplements. Vitamin K2 (MK-7) 100-200 mcg/day directs calcium to bone. Dairy, sardines with bones, kale, bok choy. Limit sodium (increases calcium excretion). Weight-bearing exercise essential.

entries.recommendation: Reduced mitochondrial superoxide dismutase activity. Higher dietary antioxidant intake needed. Emphasize colorful fruits/vegetables, green tea, dark chocolate. CoQ10 100-200 mg/day.
entries.text_chunk: SOD2 rs4880 TT (Ala/Ala) nutrition: Reduced mitochondrial antioxidant defense. High dietary antioxidant intake: berries, leafy greens, green tea, dark chocolate 85%+. CoQ10 100-200 mg/day. Avoid excess iron supplements, fried foods, charred meats. Rainbow diet for diverse phytonutrients.

entries.recommendation: Reduced diamine oxidase (DAO) activity. Limit high-histamine foods. Symptoms: headaches, flushing, GI distress after histamine-rich meals. DAO enzyme supplement before high-histamine meals.
entries.text_chunk: AOC1 rs10156191 AA nutrition: Reduced DAO enzyme, impaired histamine degradation. Limit high-histamine foods: aged cheese, fermented foods, cured meats, canned fish, wine, beer. Eat proteins fresh (histamine rises with storage). DAO enzyme supplement before high-histamine meals. Fresh fruits/vegetables, rice, fresh herbs.

entries.recommendation: ~30-40% of population carries DQ2/DQ8 but only 1-3% develop celiac disease. Not a diagnostic genotype but enables risk assessment. Screen with anti-tTG IgA if GI symptoms or autoimmune thyroiditis.
food_sources: If celiac confirmed: gluten-free grains (rice, quinoa, buckwheat, millet, amaranth)
Naturally GF: meats, fish, eggs, dairy, fruits, vegetables, legumes
avoid: Wheat, barley, rye (only if celiac confirmed)
Oats unless certified GF (only if celiac)
entries.text_chunk: HLA-DQ2/DQ8 nutrition: 30-40% carry DQ2/DQ8; 1-3% develop celiac. Not diagnostic alone. Screen anti-tTG IgA if: GI symptoms, autoimmune thyroiditis, iron deficiency, family history. If celiac confirmed: strict gluten-free. GF grains: rice, quinoa, buckwheat, millet. Negative DQ2/DQ8 essentially rules out celiac.

entries.recommendation: GSTM1 null (homozygous deletion in ~50% of population) reduces glutathione-S-transferase detoxification. Higher cruciferous vegetable intake upregulates alternative detox pathways via Nrf2.
avoid: Over-reliance on supplements vs whole food cruciferous
entries.text_chunk: GSTM1 null/null nutrition: ~50% of population has homozygous GSTM1 deletion reducing phase II detoxification. Cruciferous vegetables 5-7 servings/week upregulate alternative Nrf2 pathways. Broccoli sprouts (highest sulforaphane). Lightly steam (retains myrosinase enzyme). Broccoli, Brussels sprouts, cauliflower, kale.

entries.recommendation: Wilson disease carriers (1 in 90 people). Generally asymptomatic but may have mildly impaired copper handling. Avoid excess copper supplementation. Monitor ceruloplasmin if liver enzymes elevated.
food_sources: zinc-rich foods (oysters, beef, pumpkin seeds) help balance copper
entries.text_chunk: ATP7B heterozygous (Wilson carrier) nutrition: 1 in 90 carrier rate. Generally asymptomatic. Avoid copper supplements. Zinc-rich foods (oysters, beef, pumpkin seeds) help balance copper. Monitor ceruloplasmin if liver issues. No copper cookware if symptomatic. Varied diet provides adequate copper without excess.

entries.recommendation: Naturally higher HDL from lower CETP activity. Total cholesterol may appear elevated due to high HDL. Calculate non-HDL cholesterol or use ApoB for true atherogenic risk assessment.
food_sources: No specific dietary modification needed for CETP AA
avoid: Over-treating based on total cholesterol without HDL consideration
entries.text_chunk: CETP rs708272 AA nutrition: Naturally higher HDL +5-10 mg/dL. Total cholesterol may appear high due to HDL. Use ApoB or non-HDL cholesterol for true risk. No specific dietary CETP modification needed. Heart-healthy Mediterranean diet adequate. Do not over-restrict fat based on total cholesterol alone.

entries.recommendation: Slow COMT (Met/Met) has higher catecholamine and estrogen levels. Support methylation with adequate magnesium, B vitamins. Cruciferous vegetables promote estrogen metabolism (DIM/I3C). Limit catecholamine stimulants.
entries.text_chunk: COMT rs4680 AA (Met/Met, slow COMT) nutrition: Slower catecholamine and estrogen clearance. Cruciferous vegetables for DIM/I3C estrogen metabolism. Green tea (L-theanine) for calming. Limit caffeine (slow catecholamine clearance). Avoid high-tyramine foods. Magnesium-rich foods. B vitamin support for methylation. Consider DIM supplement 100-200 mg/day.

entries.recommendation: Higher baseline IL-6 production increases inflammatory burden. Anti-inflammatory dietary pattern essential: omega-3 rich, turmeric/curcumin, reduce omega-6 excess, limit processed foods.
avoid: Seed oils high in omega-6 (soybean, corn, sunflower)
entries.text_chunk: IL6 rs1800795 CC nutrition: Higher baseline IL-6 production. Anti-inflammatory diet critical: omega-3 rich fish 3-4x/week, turmeric with piperine, ginger, EVOO. Reduce omega-6 seed oils. No processed foods, refined sugar, trans fats. Target hs-CRP <1.0 mg/L. Berries, leafy greens, walnuts for polyphenols.

entries.recommendation: Adequate iodine essential for T4 substrate production (DIO2 AA has impaired T4-to-T3 conversion). Iodine 150-300 mcg/day. Not too much (excess iodine can worsen Hashimoto's).
food_sources: seaweed (nori, kelp - careful with kelp, very high)
avoid: Iodine-free specialty salts as sole salt source
entries.text_chunk: DIO2 rs225014 AA iodine nutrition: Adequate T4 substrate needed (impaired conversion). Iodine 150-300 mcg/day. Iodized salt, nori, dairy, eggs. CAUTION: excess iodine (>600 mcg/day) worsens Hashimoto's. Avoid kelp supplements (dangerously high iodine). Pair with selenium 200 mcg/day for thyroid optimization.

entries.recommendation: FUT2 non-secretor. Different gut microbiome composition with lower Bifidobacterium. Higher prebiotic fiber and fermented foods to support microbiome diversity.
food_sources: prebiotic-rich foods (garlic, onion, leeks, asparagus, banana)
entries.text_chunk: FUT2 rs601338 AA (non-secretor) nutrition: Lower Bifidobacterium in gut. Higher prebiotic fiber 30-50 g/day. Garlic, onion, leeks, asparagus for prebiotics. Fermented foods (yogurt, kefir, sauerkraut). Aim 30+ plant species/week for diversity. Resistant starch from cooled rice/potatoes.

entries.recommendation: Reduced beta-carotene conversion to retinol. Higher need for preformed vitamin A (retinol) and direct lutein/zeaxanthin for macular health. Cannot rely on beta-carotene alone.
food_sources: sweet potatoes with fat for absorption
avoid: Relying solely on beta-carotene supplements for vitamin A needs
entries.text_chunk: BCMO1 rs12934922 TT nutrition: Poor beta-carotene converter. Need preformed vitamin A (retinol from liver, eggs) and direct lutein/zeaxanthin. Egg yolks (2/day, most bioavailable lutein). Kale, spinach, orange peppers. Cannot rely on beta-carotene supplements alone. Important for macular health.

entries.recommendation: Higher 4-OH estrogen metabolite production (more genotoxic). Support 2-OH pathway with cruciferous vegetables (DIM/I3C). Adequate methylation (MTHFR status dependent) for COMT-mediated estrogen clearance.
entries.text_chunk: CYP1B1 rs1056836 CC nutrition: Higher 4-OH estrogen (genotoxic). Cruciferous vegetables 5+ servings/week for DIM/I3C (favors 2-OH pathway). Flaxseed 2 tbsp/day (lignans). Green tea, turmeric, rosemary. Avoid charred meats (PAHs upregulate CYP1B1), excess alcohol. Target 2-OH:16-OH ratio >2.0.

entries.recommendation: MTHFR AA depletes glycine through impaired one-carbon metabolism. Supplemental glycine 3-5 g/day or collagen peptides 10-15 g/day. Supports glutathione production and methylation balance.
avoid: Glycine depletion from high methionine diet without balancing
entries.text_chunk: MTHFR AA glycine nutrition: Impaired one-carbon metabolism depletes glycine. Glycine 3-5 g/day or collagen peptides 10-15 g/day. Bone broth (2+ cups/week), collagen in coffee/smoothie. Supports glutathione production. Balance methionine:glycine ratio. Supports sleep, gut lining, and methylation.

entries.recommendation: Modified estrogen receptor sensitivity. Moderate soy isoflavone intake may be beneficial (1-2 servings/day). Monitor thyroid function if high soy intake with DIO2 AA genotype.
food_sources: organic non-GMO soy foods (tofu, tempeh, edamame)
entries.text_chunk: ESR1 rs2234693 TT nutrition: Modified estrogen receptor sensitivity. Moderate soy beneficial (1-2 servings/day): tofu, tempeh, edamame. Organic non-GMO preferred. Flaxseed lignans. Monitor thyroid if combining high soy with DIO2 AA genotype. Avoid soy protein isolates (concentrated).

entries.recommendation: Caution with high-saturated-fat ketogenic diets. APOE4 carriers have exaggerated LDL response to saturated fat. If ketogenic diet desired, use modified Mediterranean-keto (MUFA-based) with olive oil, avocado, nuts, fish.
avoid: Standard ketogenic diet high in butter/coconut oil/cream
entries.text_chunk: APOE E4/E4 ketogenic diet: CAUTION with standard high-saturated-fat keto. Use MUFA-based Mediterranean-keto instead: avocado, olive oil, fatty fish, nuts. Avoid butter/coconut oil/cream-heavy keto. Monitor LDL closely (check at 4 weeks). LDL >200 on keto = discontinue or modify. Target LDL <100.

entries.recommendation: Reduced SIRT1 expression. Dietary polyphenols activate SIRT1 pathway. Resveratrol from food sources, quercetin, EGCG from green tea. Caloric restriction or time-restricted eating also activates SIRT1.
entries.text_chunk: SIRT1 rs7895833 GG nutrition: Reduced SIRT1 expression. Dietary polyphenol activation: red grapes, berries (resveratrol), green tea (EGCG), onions (quercetin), EVOO (oleocanthal), dark chocolate. Time-restricted eating (16:8) activates SIRT1. Do NOT drink red wine for resveratrol (alcohol risks > benefits). Target PhenoAge acceleration ≤0.

entries.recommendation: Impaired zinc transport into insulin granules. Adequate zinc intake 15-30 mg/day from food and supplements. Zinc supports insulin packaging and secretion in beta cells.
entries.text_chunk: SLC30A8 rs13266634 CC nutrition: Impaired ZnT8 zinc transport into insulin granules. Zinc 15-30 mg/day (picolinate or glycinate form). Oysters (best food source), beef, pumpkin seeds, lentils. Supports insulin packaging and secretion. Do not exceed 40 mg/day (copper depletion). Target serum zinc 80-120 ug/dL.

entries.recommendation: Curcumin 500-1000 mg/day with piperine for hepatoprotection. Anti-inflammatory and anti-fibrotic properties particularly relevant for PNPLA3 GG liver risk.
food_sources: turmeric with black pepper (piperine enhances absorption 2000%)
avoid: Curcumin supplements if on blood thinners (may enhance effect)
Turmeric supplements with liver disease without physician guidance
entries.text_chunk: PNPLA3 GG curcumin nutrition: Curcumin 500-1000 mg/day with piperine for hepatoprotection. Anti-inflammatory and anti-fibrotic properties. Golden milk (turmeric + coconut milk + black pepper). Part of comprehensive liver-protective strategy with Mediterranean diet and alcohol avoidance. Caution with blood thinners.

entries.recommendation: Reduced taurine synthesis capacity. Taurine important for bile acid conjugation, cardiac function, and antioxidant defense. Supplement 1-3 g/day or emphasize taurine-rich foods.
food_sources: energy drinks (synthetic taurine, but not recommended due to other ingredients)
avoid: Vegan diet without taurine supplementation (no plant taurine sources)
entries.text_chunk: CSAD rs7422339 AA nutrition: Reduced taurine synthesis. Taurine 1-3 g/day supplement or shellfish (scallops, mussels, clams), dark meat poultry, fish. Important for bile acids, cardiac function, antioxidant defense. Vegans must supplement (no plant taurine). Do not use energy drinks as taurine source.

entries.recommendation: Mediterranean diet shows benefit across virtually all genotypes studied. Particularly beneficial for: APOE E4 (lipids), TCF7L2 TT (glucose), PNPLA3 GG (liver), GCKR TT (triglycerides), IL6 CC (inflammation).
entries.text_chunk: Mediterranean Diet across genotypes: Universal benefit confirmed in PREDIMED, DPP, and observational studies. APOE E4: lipid improvement. TCF7L2 TT: glucose control. PNPLA3 GG: liver protection. GCKR TT: triglyceride management. IL6 CC: anti-inflammatory. Core: olive oil 4+ tbsp/day, vegetables 4+ servings, fish 3+/week, legumes 3+/week, nuts daily.

entries.recommendation: CLOCK CC may have disrupted circadian rhythm affecting meal timing response. Time-restricted eating (10-12 hour window) rather than strict 16:8. Morning-weighted caloric intake preferred.
avoid: Late-night eating (impaired glucose tolerance after 8 PM)
entries.text_chunk: CLOCK rs1801260 CC nutrition: Disrupted circadian rhythm. Time-restricted eating 10-12 hour window (not strict 16:8). Front-load calories: larger breakfast and lunch, lighter dinner. No eating 3 hours before bed. Late-night eating particularly harmful (impaired glucose tolerance). Morning-weighted eating pattern.

entries.recommendation: NQO1 TT has reduced ubiquinone-to-ubiquinol conversion. Use ubiquinol form (not ubiquinone) of CoQ10, 200-400 mg/day. Important if on statins (which deplete CoQ10).
avoid: Ubiquinone form of CoQ10 (use ubiquinol instead for TT genotype)
entries.text_chunk: NQO1 rs1800566 TT nutrition: Impaired ubiquinone-to-ubiquinol conversion. Use UBIQUINOL form of CoQ10 200-400 mg/day (not ubiquinone). Especially important if on statins. Organ meats (heart), sardines, beef for dietary CoQ10. Target plasma CoQ10 1.0-3.0 ug/mL.

entries.recommendation: Evidence-based longevity nutrition: caloric optimization (not excess), high polyphenol intake, adequate protein (1.2-1.6 g/kg), omega-3 index >8%, vitamin D >40 ng/mL, minimize ultra-processed foods. Targets PhenoAge and GrimAge biomarker optimization.
entries.text_chunk: Comprehensive longevity nutrition: Caloric optimization, high polyphenol rainbow diet, protein 1.2-1.6 g/kg, omega-3 index >8%, vitamin D >40 ng/mL. Blue Zone staples: legumes, nuts, olive oil. Green tea, fermented foods, herbs. Minimize ultra-processed foods (accelerate PhenoAge). Targets: PhenoAge acceleration ≤0 years, optimize all 9 PhenoAge biomarkers. GrimAge protein surrogates also diet-responsive.
